items.headline: Auto sales use proposed for El Camino Real commercial site
items.summary: The Planning Commission will consider a special development permit to allow auto sales at 975 W. El Camino Real. The proposal involves site improvements like repaving and landscaping but no building expansion or exterior changes.

items.headline: 195-unit apartment complex permitted near Berryessa transit station
items.summary: A $32 million multi-family development at 825 Berryessa Station Way has received its building permit, adding 195 units of housing near the Berryessa BART station. The project represents significant transit-oriented development in the Berryessa neighborhood.

items.headline: Financial assistance pilot program launches for water customers
items.summary: City Council approved a new Financial Assistance Pilot Program to help water customers manage costs. The program aims to provide relief to residents facing water bill challenges.

items.headline: City Council to discuss potential litigation from Cuesta Park water main incident
items.summary: Mountain View City Council held a closed session on May 12 to confer with legal counsel about potential litigation arising from a water main incident at Cuesta Park. The council also discussed real property negotiations for properties at 485 and 495 Clyde Avenue.

items.headline: Planning Commission considers 27-unit townhome project with affordable housing
items.summary: The Planning Commission reviewed a residential development proposal for 27 three-story townhomes, including 5 affordable units. The project represents a substantive housing addition to Cupertino's residential stock with an inclusionary affordable component.

items.headline: Litigation update: Fantozzi case against city and SummerHill Homes
items.summary: The City Council received a closed-session report on Mark Fantozzi v. City of Cupertino and SummerHill Homes, LLC (Case No. 26CV492787), though the substance of the litigation and any council action remain confidential under state law.

items.headline: City considers 72-unit affordable housing project on Lytton Avenue
items.summary: The City Council held a public hearing on a proposal to build a six-story, 72-unit apartment building at 450 Lytton Avenue entirely for low-income residents. The project would replace a city-owned surface parking lot and requests density bonuses under state affordable housing law.

items.headline: Campbell adopts policy restricting use of city property for federal civil enforcement
items.summary: The City Council adopted a resolution prohibiting city-owned and city-operated property from being used for federal civil enforcement actions. This policy limits how federal agencies can access or utilize municipal facilities and resources.
